name: raffle-winner-picker
description: Picks random winners from lists, spreadsheets, or Google Sheets for giveaways, raffles, and contests. Ensures fair, unbiased selection with transparency.
capabilities: Select random winners from participant lists using cryptographic algorithms
Generate verifiable random selections with seed-based reproducibility
Support multiple winner selection with duplicate prevention
Handle various input formats including CSV and plain text lists
author: ComposioHQ
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 26 patterns including cryptographic functions and shell commands. Upon evaluation, all findings are false positives. The MD5 usage is for non-security purposes (generating random seeds), shell backticks are example code snippets in documentation, and C2/reconnaissance keywords are normal JSON field names in a skill report file.
valueStatement: Running contests and giveaways requires transparent random selection. This skill uses cryptographic randomization to select winners fairly from participant lists.
limitations: Cannot verify participant eligibility or detect fraudulent entries
Does not integrate with social media platforms for automatic entry collection
Cannot handle real-time streaming entry collection during live events

useCases.title: Instagram Giveaway Winner Selection
useCases.description: Pick random winners from Instagram comment participants for product giveaways and promotional contests

useCases.title: Conference Door Prize Drawing
useCases.description: Conduct transparent random draws for conference attendees to win prizes and sponsorship gifts

useCases.title: Discord Server Nitro Giveaway
useCases.description: Randomly select community members from participant lists for Discord Nitro and other digital rewards

promptTemplates.title: Basic Winner Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: Pick one random winner from this list of participants: Alice, Bob, Charlie, Diana, Edward

promptTemplates.title: Multiple Winners
promptTemplates.prompt: Select 3 random winners from my participant list for first, second, and third place prizes. Ensure no duplicates.

promptTemplates.title: Seeded Random Draw
promptTemplates.prompt: Pick 2 winners from this CSV file using seed 12345 so I can verify the results later

promptTemplates.title: Large Scale Raffle
promptTemplates.prompt: I have 5000 participants in participants.txt. Select 10 unique winners and show me the complete selection process for transparency.

bestPractices: Always announce the selection method and seed value before conducting the raffle for transparency
Save the complete participant list and random seed to allow independent verification of results
Conduct the random selection publicly or record the process to build trust with participants
antiPatterns: Do not modify the participant list after announcing the raffle deadline to maintain fairness
Avoid running multiple selections without documenting each attempt as this appears manipulative
Never manually override random results based on preferred outcomes as this defeats the purpose

faq.question: How does the random selection ensure fairness?
faq.answer: The skill uses cryptographic random number generation to eliminate bias and ensure each participant has equal probability of winning.

faq.question: Can I verify the raffle results were truly random?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides a random seed that allows anyone to reproduce the exact same selection results independently.

faq.question: What happens if I have duplicate names in my list?
faq.answer: Each entry is treated separately. If you want unique participants only, remove duplicates from your input list first.

faq.question: Can I pick multiple winners without replacement?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports selecting multiple winners and ensures no participant is selected twice in the same draw.

faq.question: What input formats are supported for participant lists?
faq.answer: The skill accepts comma-separated lists, CSV files, and plain text files with one participant per line.

faq.question: Is the selection method legally compliant for official contests?
faq.answer: The randomization is cryptographically sound, but you should consult local regulations for specific contest compliance requirements.
